rationale: The citing paper uses Shachar and Emerson (2000) in its theory framing to support a specific prior finding: continued viewing in TV contexts is linked to program continuity/storyline (e.g., drama vs. news or sports). The authors then position their core contribution as extending that prior TV-series based pattern to stand-alone, divisible media and providing causal evidence via experiments. Although often co-cited with other papers, this citation helps define the research gap and the paper's claimed advance, so it is more than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Shachar and Emerson (2000) as prior TV-viewing evidence and extends that line by testing whether similar sequential-consumption persistence also occurs for stand-alone media without a shared narrative structure.
